title: Stocks for the Long Run
subtitle: The Definitive Guide to Financial Market Returns & Long-Term Investment Strategy
author: Jeremy J. Siegel
themes: Stocks outperform bonds by 2-3x over any 30-year rolling period in US history
The equity risk premium exists and persists — stocks offer 4-7% excess return over bonds historically
Inflation devastates fixed-income assets; stocks are the best long-term inflation hedge
Long investment horizons eliminate short-term volatility risk and unlock compounding
The Siegel constant: long-run real stock return never falls below 5.5% including dividends
Market timing fails — the bulk of returns comes from a small number of best days
Dividend reinvestment is responsible for the majority of long-run stock returns
Bond allocations should scale with age and risk tolerance; equities dominate young portfolios
International diversification provides real benefits beyond domestic-only strategies
Contemporary headwinds suggest future real stock returns may be below the long-run average
awards: Named among the best investing books of all time by multiple outlets
Widely used as a textbook at business schools including Wharton and Stanford
Endorsed by leading institutional investors and academics
Sustained influence across 6 editions over nearly 30 years
excerpt: Over two centuries of data, stocks have yielded a real return of roughly 7% annually versus under 2% for bonds — a gap of more than three to one. Jeremy J. Siegel's landmark work is the definitive statistical history of financial market returns and the case for staying invested through every cycle.
